title: Case 92866: College Decision 025/26/COL - Closure decision - Norway - Complaint - draft prohibition on domestic distance selling of tobacco products
text:
Case No: 92866
Document No: 1577315
Decision No: 025/26/COL
EFTA SURVEILLANCE AUTHORITY DECISION
of 11 February 2026
closing a complaint against the Norwegian Government concerning draft prohibitions
on domestic distance selling of tobacco products
THE EFTA SURVEILLANCE AUTHORITY
Having regard to the Agreement between the EFTA States on the Establishment of a
Surveillance Authority and a Court of Justice, in particular Article 31 thereof,
Whereas:
On 1 October 2024, the EFTA Surveillance Authority (“the Authority”) received a complaint
alleging that draft legislation that was the subject of a public hearing in Norway in 2024 was
contrary to EEA law. The draft legislation concerned prohibitions on cross-border and
domestic distance selling of tobacco products and electronic cigarettes.
The complaint only concerns the part of the draft legislation that concerns domestic
distance selling of tobacco products. The complainant argues that this part of the draft
legislation would be contrary to the right of establishment under EEA law, as well as to the
Tobacco Products Directive, the Services Directive and the e-Commerce Directive.
Given that the complaint targets draft legislation which has not yet been adopted, the
Authority’s Internal Market Affairs Directorate (“the Directorate”) has not taken any formal
action but limited itself to follow the legislative process in Norway.
In September 2025, the Directorate met with representatives of the Norwegian Ministry of
Health and Care Services to discuss questions concerning notifying the Authority under the
Single Market Transparency Directive about draft legislation aiming to prohibit distance
selling of tobacco products. In this context, which was separate from the present complaint
case, the Ministry explained that the Norwegian Government has decided to proceed only
with the part of its proposal that concerns cross-border distance selling, and not with the
part that concerns distance selling within Norway. The Ministry has later confirmed this.
The complaint concerns a draft prohibition on domestic distance selling of tobacco
products. Given that the Norwegian Government has confirmed that its work on this draft
prohibition has been discontinued, the Authority considers that the issue raised in the
complaint has been resolved and that the case can therefore be closed.
By letter of 24 October 2025, the Directorate informed the complainant of its intention to
propose to the Authority that the case be closed. The complainant was invited to submit
Avenue des Arts 19H, B-1000 Brussels, tel: +32 2 286 18 11, www.eftasurv.int

Page 2
any observations on the Directorate’s assessment of the complaint or present any new
information by 24 November 2025.
The complainant did not reply to that letter.
There are, therefore, no grounds for pursuing this case further.
HAS ADOPTED THIS DECISION:
The complaint case concerning draft prohibitions on domestic distance selling of tobacco
products is hereby closed.
For the EFTA Surveillance Authority
Arne Røksund Árni Páll Árnason Nuscha Wieczorek
President Responsible College Member College Member
Melpo-Menie Joséphidès
Countersigning as Director,
Legal and Executive Affairs
This document has been electronically authenticated by Arne Roeksund, Melpo-Menie
Josephides.
